Title: Add New Catalog
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Catalog module accessible
Steps:
[NAV] Open Catalog module > Add > Click Catalog
[ASSERT] Verify screen displays with required fields
[ACT] Fill Catalog name field (required)
[ACT] Configure Language block if needed
[ACT] Click Create
Assertions:
[STATE] System displays new catalog screen
[STATE] Catalog name field marked as required
[STATE] Language block visible and functional
[STATE] System saves new catalog successfully
[DOM] New catalog appears in catalog list
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/catalogs — verify new catalog returned
[CONSOLE] No errors on create
Failure_Signals: Catalog save fails silently; required field validation not shown; duplicate catalog name accepted

Title: Edit Existing Catalog
Preconditions: Admin logged in, at least one catalog exists
Steps:
[NAV] Right-click on existing catalog > Manage
[ASSERT] Verify edit screen displays
[ACT] Review blocks: Name (required), Language, Property
[ACT] Modify catalog name
[ACT] Change language settings
[ACT] Modify property settings
[ACT] Click Save
Assertions:
[STATE] Edit screen displays with catalog data
[STATE] Name field is required
[STATE] Language block shows current selection
[STATE] Property block displays correctly
[STATE] System saves all changes
[DOM] Updated catalog reflects changes
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/catalogs/{id} — verify updated fields
[CONSOLE] No errors on save
Failure_Signals: Save button inactive after edits; name not persisted after save; language block missing
Cleanup: restore original catalog name via Admin

Title: Delete Catalog - Cancel
Preconditions: Admin logged in, multiple catalogs exist
Steps:
[NAV] Right-click on any catalog > Delete
[ASSERT] Verify confirmation dialog appears
[ACT] Click Cancel
Assertions:
[STATE] Confirmation dialog displays
[STATE] System does not delete catalog
[DOM] Catalog remains in list
[STATE] No data loss
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/catalogs — verify catalog still exists
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: Catalog deleted despite Cancel click; dialog not shown; list count changes

[STATE] Entering a term filters the product/category list to matching items
[STATE] Clearing the search restores the full list
[STATE] Category name search filters categories correctly
[STATE] Non-existent term shows an empty state or No results message
[STATE] Search is case-insensitive
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/search?q=TestProduct — verify results match UI
[CONSOLE] No search errors
Failure_Signals: Search not filtering; clear search not restoring full list; case-sensitive search; no empty-state message

Title: Clone BOM Product
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), a BOM (Bill of Materials) product exists with at least one component
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to Catalog module
[NAV] Locate and open a BOM product
[ASSERT] Note the product name, properties, and BOM components
[ACT] Click the Clone or Copy action (from toolbar or context menu)
[ACT] Confirm the clone action in the dialog
[WAIT] Wait for the clone to complete
[NAV] Open the cloned product from the list
Assertions:
[STATE] Clone action is available on BOM products
[STATE] Cloned product is created with a distinct name (e.g. Copy of original name)
[STATE] All top-level product properties are copied to the clone
[STATE] BOM component list is present in the cloned product
[STATE] Clone has a new unique product ID
[STATE] No errors occur during the clone operation
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/products — verify both BOM products with distinct IDs
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: Clone timeout; BOM components not cloned; clone has same ID as original; clone action not available
Cleanup: delete cloned BOM product via Admin

Title: Clone BOM Product Variations
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), a BOM product with variations (child products) exists
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to Catalog module and open a BOM product with variations
[ASSERT] Note the number and details of variations
[ACT] Initiate the Clone action
[ACT] Confirm the clone action
[NAV] Open the cloned product
[NAV] Navigate to the Variations tab of the cloned product
[ASSERT] Verify the cloned variations match the originals in count and properties
Assertions:
[STATE] Cloned BOM product includes all variations from the original
[STATE] Each variation is a new record with a unique ID
[STATE] Variation properties (name, SKU, images) are copied correctly
[STATE] Cloned variations are editable independently of the original
[STATE] No variation data is lost or corrupted during cloning
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/products/{cloneId}/variations — verify variations count and properties
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: Variations not cloned; cloned variations share IDs with originals; variation data corrupted
Cleanup: delete cloned BOM product with variations via Admin

Title: Virtual Catalog Assignment
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), a virtual catalog and at least one physical catalog product exist
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to Catalog module
[ACT] Open or create a Virtual Catalog
[NAV] Navigate into a category within the virtual catalog
[ACT] Click Add Items or Link Products
[ACT] Search for and select a product from the physical catalog
[ACT] Confirm the assignment
[ASSERT] Verify the product appears in the virtual catalog category
Assertions:
[STATE] Virtual catalog structure is accessible and editable
[STATE] Product search returns physical catalog products
[STATE] Selected product is assigned to the virtual catalog category
[DOM] Assigned product is visible in the virtual catalog without duplicating the original
[STATE] Assignment persists after page refresh
[STATE] Product remains in its original physical catalog
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/virtualcatalogs/{id} — verify product assignment
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: Product duplicated instead of linked; assignment lost after refresh; product removed from physical catalog
Cleanup: remove virtual catalog assignment via Admin

Title: Dynamic Product Associations
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), Associations feature enabled, multiple products exist
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to Catalog module and open a product
[NAV] Navigate to the Associations tab
[ACT] Click Add Dynamic Association or configure an association group
[ACT] Define association criteria (e.g. same category, same brand)
[ACT] Save the association rule
[ASSERT] Verify the associated products list populates based on the rule
[ASSERT] Verify associated products update if catalog changes match the rule
Assertions:
[STATE] Associations tab is accessible on the product blade
[STATE] Dynamic association rule can be created with criteria
[STATE] Matching products are listed under the dynamic association
[STATE] Association list refreshes when matching criteria are met
[STATE] Saved rules persist after navigating away and returning
[STATE] No performance degradation with large product sets
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/products/{id}/associations — verify dynamic association results
[CONSOLE] No performance errors
Failure_Signals: Association tab missing; rules not saving; associated list empty despite matching products; timeout with large sets
Cleanup: delete created association rule via Admin

Title: Product Required Properties Validation
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), catalog schema has at least one property marked as Required
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to Catalog module and open or create a product
[NAV] Navigate to the Properties tab
[ASSERT] Identify a property marked as Required (indicated by asterisk or label)
[ACT] Clear or leave the required property field empty
[ACT] Attempt to Save the product
[ASSERT] Observe validation feedback
[ACT] Fill in the required property and save again
Assertions:
[STATE] Required properties are visually marked (asterisk or highlight)
[STATE] Saving with empty required property shows a validation error message
[DOM] Error message identifies which property is missing
[STATE] Product does not save while required fields are empty
[STATE] After filling required field, product saves successfully
[STATE] No data loss occurs for non-required fields during failed save attempt
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] PUT /api/catalog/products — verify 400 response when required property missing
[CONSOLE] No unhandled errors
[DOM] Validation error visible in UI
Failure_Signals: No visual marking for required properties; product saves without required property; error message vague

Title: Text Option Properties
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), Catalog module with property management access
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to Catalog module > Properties or open a catalog property blade
[ACT] Click Add Property
[ACT] Select ShortText or Text as the property value type
[ACT] Set property display name and configure it as an Option (not free-text)
[ACT] Add at least 3 predefined option values
[ACT] Save the property
[ACT] Assign it to a product and verify the option values appear as a dropdown
Assertions:
[STATE] Property is created with text type and predefined options
[STATE] Option values are saved and listed correctly
[DOM] On a product, the property appears as a dropdown with the defined options
[STATE] Only predefined options are selectable (no free-text entry)
[STATE] Selecting an option and saving the product persists the value
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/properties — verify property with options
[API] GET /api/catalog/products/{id} — verify option value persisted
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: Free-text entry allowed despite option type; options not shown in dropdown; option value not saved

Title: Property Name Rules Validation
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), Catalog module with property creation access
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to Catalog module > Properties blade
[ACT] Click Add Property
[ACT] Attempt to enter invalid characters in the property Name field (e.g. spaces, special chars like @#!%)
[ASSERT] Observe whether validation prevents saving
[ACT] Attempt to save the property with an invalid name
[ASSERT] Observe the error message
[ACT] Enter a valid property name (alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens only)
[ACT] Save and verify success
Assertions:
[STATE] Invalid characters in property name trigger a validation error
[DOM] Error message clearly states the name rules (e.g. Only alphanumeric, underscore, and hyphen allowed)
[STATE] Property cannot be saved with an invalid name
[STATE] Valid name allows the property to be created successfully
[STATE] Existing properties with valid names are not affected
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST /api/catalog/properties — verify 400 response for invalid name
[CONSOLE] No unhandled errors
[DOM] Validation error visible in UI
Failure_Signals: Invalid name accepted; no error message; error message not descriptive; valid name rejected
Cleanup: delete created property (from valid save) via Admin
